meta.title: St. Mark Coptic Academy — Your Accredited Gateway to Theology & Coptic Studies
meta.description: The leading academic reference in ecclesiastical education and scholarly research, under the patronage of His Holiness Pope Tawadros II. Accredited Master's, Doctorate and Diploma programs, a theses digital library, and a peer-reviewed academic journal.

hero.title: St. Mark Coptic Academy
hero.subtitle: Your accredited academic gateway to theology, Coptic studies and scholarly research,
hero.imageAlt: St. Mark Coptic Academy campus at the Logos Center

vision.label: Our Vision
vision.subtitle: What is our goal for the future?
vision.body: To be the leading, integrated academic reference in ecclesiastical education and church-based scholarly research — uniting theological colleges and ecclesiastical institutes under one umbrella; safeguarding the Apostolic Tradition and preserving the Patristic heritage; strengthening Coptic identity and affirming its presence in the modern era; serving the Church and society through innovative education, research and creativity; and enriching cultural and scholarly dialogue locally, regionally and internationally — drawing inspiration from the spirit of the early Christian School of Alexandria.
vision.imageAlt1: Academic conference in the grand lecture hall
vision.imageAlt2: Manuscripts and references from the academy library
vision.visionHeadline: The leading reference in ecclesiastical education
vision.missionHeadline: Forming servants and scholars for the Church
vision.missionBody: To provide integrated academic and spiritual formation rooted in the Apostolic Tradition — preparing clergy, teachers and researchers through rigorous theological study, to preserve the Patristic heritage, renew Coptic identity, and serve the Church and society with faith, scholarship and love.

items.label: thousand m² at the Logos Center

programs.label: Academic Programs
programs.subtitle: Accredited degrees for every stage of your journey

items.title: MA in Theology & Coptic Studies
items.description: An in-depth research degree in dogmatic and biblical theology and patristics, supervised by distinguished specialized professors.

items.title: PhD in Theological Studies
items.description: The highest academic degree for researchers in Coptic heritage and patristic theology, with peer-reviewed publication.

items.title: Specialized Diplomas
items.description: Focused programs in theology, church history, the Coptic language and ecclesial education.

items.title: Foundational Certificates
items.description: Short accredited courses for servants and new students, on campus or online via the TEACH platform.

theses.label: Theses Digital Library
theses.subtitle: Accredited Master's and Doctoral theses
theses.heroSubtitle: Accredited Master's and doctoral theses in theology, patristics, Coptic studies, and church history — from the Academy and its partner institutions.

items.title: The Theological School of Alexandria and Its Impact on Early Christian Thought
The Theology of the Incarnation in St. Athanasius the Apostolic — A Patristic Study
Coptic Art in the Byzantine Era — A Documentary Analytical Reading
The Liturgy of St. Mark — Its Origin and Textual Development
Official Accreditation
items.summary: Certificates authenticated by the Holy Synod
items.body: Academy certificates are issued authenticated and accredited by the Holy Synod of the Coptic Orthodox Church, guaranteeing ecclesiastical and academic recognition of your degrees in Egypt and abroad.

items.title: Faculty
items.summary: Metropolitans, bishops and internationally specialized professors
items.body: Programs are supervised by a distinguished body of metropolitans, bishops and university professors specialized in theology and Coptic studies from Egypt and around the world — combining academic rigor with ecclesial experience.

items.title: Infrastructure
items.summary: Equipped halls, a digital library and conference centers
items.body: A fully integrated campus at the Logos Center in Wadi El-Natrun: equipped lecture halls, a vast physical and digital library, and conference centers hosting international scholarly events.

items.title: Hybrid Learning
items.summary: On campus or online via the international TEACH platform
items.body: Study the way that suits you: on campus, or remotely via the international TEACH platform with live interactive lectures and a recordings library available around the clock.

articles.label: Articles & Publications
articles.subtitle: From the academy journal and periodicals

items.title: The Spirit of the School of Alexandria: From Pantaenus to Origen
items.excerpt: How did the first theological school in Christian history shape ecclesial education to this day?
items.author: Dr. Basilios Sobhy

items.title: Coptic Manuscripts of Wadi El-Natrun: Treasures Beneath the Sands
items.excerpt: A documentary journey through the monastery manuscripts that preserved the patristic heritage across sixteen centuries.
items.author: Prof. Marina Nabil

items.title: Theological Research Methodology in the Digital Age
items.excerpt: Modern tools, databases and methods opening new horizons for the contemporary church researcher.
items.author: Dr. Youhanna Fawzy

articles.emptyState: No published articles yet — check back soon.

items.name: Mariam Atef
items.quote: The academy gave me an integrated research environment that changed my academic path — precise supervision and rich scholarly resources.

items.name: Mina Adel
items.quote: The academy doesn't just teach — it understands the student's mission and nurtures the spirit of research and service together.

items.name: Marina Samir
items.quote: From day one the experience was organized and clear — rigorous lectures and continuous follow-up from supervisors.

items.name: Karin Wadie
items.quote: I benefited from the scholarly archive from my first week, finding rare references I couldn't locate anywhere else.

items.name: Kyrillos William
items.quote: I only realized how much scholarly methodology I was missing after joining the academy; my whole way of thinking changed.

items.name: Daniel Samuel
items.quote: I finally found a place that unites the depth of patristic heritage with the modern tools of education.

items.name: Clara Morcos
items.quote: From registration to the thesis defense, attention to detail was evident at every step.

items.name: Eva Nashy
items.quote: The academy helped me launch into research with confidence, thanks to foundational courses and academic advising.

items.name: Morcos Fawzy
items.quote: It is rare to find an institution balancing academic rigor with authentic ecclesial spirit so well.

items.name: Rachel Fouad
items.quote: Every step was well designed — precise questions and guidance that exceeded expectations at every stage.

items.name: Abanoub Fayez
items.quote: Despite the tight schedule of my final semester, I found all the support to finish my thesis on time.

items.name: Teresa Younan
items.quote: It wasn't just study — it was a transformation of thought. I'm proud to belong to this institution.

faq.label: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Registration steps — what, how and why?
faq.noResults: No results match your search — try different keywords.

items.question: How do I register at the academy?
items.answer: Create an account on the platform, then fill in the admission form and attach your documents (academic qualification and ID). You'll receive a preliminary acceptance email within 48 working hours, followed by a short introductory interview.

items.question: What are the admission requirements for postgraduate programs?
items.answer: A recognized university degree, a church recommendation from your priest or spiritual guide, and passing the personal interview. The PhD program requires a Master's degree or its equivalent.

items.question: Are the certificates accredited?
items.answer: Yes — all certificates are issued authenticated and accredited by the Holy Synod of the Coptic Orthodox Church, carry the signature of His Holiness the Pope, and are recognized by partner theological colleges and institutes.

items.question: What are the tuition payment methods?
items.answer: You can pay by bank transfer, online payment through the platform, or in cash at the academy — with the option of installments per semester. Partial scholarships are available for servants and distinguished researchers.

items.question: Can I study remotely?
items.answer: Yes — the academy offers hybrid learning via the international TEACH platform: live interactive lectures, recordings available around the clock, and optional intensive on-campus meetings.

items.question: How does the academy advance church scholarly research?
items.answer: Through the digital theses library, a peer-reviewed academic journal, annual international conferences, specialized supervision of Master's and PhD theses, and research partnerships with theological colleges and institutes.

items.question: Are there international partnerships?
items.answer: Yes — the academy holds cooperation agreements with theological colleges and institutes in Egypt and abroad, including researcher exchange, mutual recognition of degrees, and participation in international conferences.

items.question: How do I access the theses library and articles?
items.answer: After creating your account you can search the theses library for free. Some articles are public, while exclusive content requires a modest annual subscription that supports the scholarly journal.
